title: Small Ladder Bookshelf
summary: Three open plywood-bottomed trays leaning against an angled 1x2 ladder frame — a small leaning bookshelf sized for a nightstand or kids' room.
description:
The three trays are graduated in size from top to bottom, and each is really just a shallow open box with a plywood bottom — nothing complicated there. The ladder frame is what carries the visual interest and the mild engineering challenge: the two long rails are cut at a 10° angle so the whole thing leans against a wall by design rather than standing bolt upright, and the shorter mounting pieces get a matching 10° angle on one end to seat flush against those leaning rails.

Build the leg/rail assembly in mirror image on both sides — drilling the pocket holes on opposite faces so they end up hidden on the inside once the ladder frame is assembled, the same technique used on any piece where two mirrored sides need to hide their joinery from each other.

tools.note: Only needed for any decorative cutouts.

cutList.note: Cut to match each tray: 9", 11", and 13-1/2" respectively.
Both ends cut at 10° off square, ends parallel — long point to short point measurement.
One end cut at 10° off square, longest point measurement.
One end cut at 10° off square, longest point measurement.

steps.title: Build the three trays
steps.body: Build the three open trays using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the 1/4" plywood bottoms to the underside of each tray with glue and 3/4" nails or staples.

steps.title: Build the ladder legs
steps.body:
Build the ladder legs with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Build and drill the two leg assemblies as mirror images of each other, so the pocket holes end up hidden on the inside faces once assembled.

Mark the shelf locations on the inside of the legs with a square, and attach the trays with 1-1/4" screws — pocket hole screws work fine here too.

images.alt: Small leaning ladder bookshelf with three graduated trays
